_comment: GOL-254 (GOL-241 Tier 2B) — per-droplet OPT-IN manifest for guarded auto-resize.
This file IS the board decision D2 artifact. A droplet only becomes eligible
for automated CPU/RAM resize when the board adds it here AND flips its
'enabled' to true in a reviewed PR. Presence with enabled:false = codified but
still dry-run. Second key: the AUTO_RESIZE_ENABLED Actions secret must also be
'1' or the whole executor stays dry-run globally. Two keys, both human-set.
Fields per droplet:
name              DO droplet name (matched against the live DO account).
enabled           board opt-in switch. false => never mutates (dry-run only).
min_slug/max_slug hard cost/blast-radius band; the executor never resizes
outside [min_slug, max_slug] on the shared size ladder.
bump_pct          p95 CPU or mem >= this over the window => bump up one tier.
downsize_mem_pct  p95 mem below this AND cpu below downsize_cpu_pct => down.
downsize_cpu_pct  (separate up/down thresholds = the hysteresis band).
window_days       metric lookback; the breach must hold across the whole
window (sustained, not a spike).
cooldown_hours    minimum time since the LAST resize action (read from the
DO actions API — stateless) before another is allowed.
maintenance_window UTC days-of-week (0=Mon..6=Sun) and hours the ~1-2 min
power-off/resize/power-on reboot is permitted. Empty lists
=> always allowed (NOT recommended for revenue-adjacent).
health_check      url + expect_status + timeout_s; after power-on the box
must return expect_status within timeout_s or the executor
ROLLS BACK to the previous slug and announces the failure.
NEVER grows disk: every resize is issued disk=false (CPU/RAM-only, reversible).
DO disk grows are one-way, so automation is forbidden from touching disk.
